@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9f80
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Escape does not close the inspector — make the dismiss chain end-to-end actually fire
+---
+## What
+
+Pressing **Escape** with an inspector panel open is supposed to close the topmost panel. Today it doesn't. Every link of the dismiss chain exists in code and unit-tests in isolation, but no test exercises the production chain end-to-end, so the regression slipped in undetected.
+
+The chain, as currently designed:
+
+1. **Keybinding handler** (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx:46` `KeybindingHandler` → `createKeyHandler`). On Escape, walks the focused scope chain via `extractScopeBindings`, finds the first command bound to `Escape`. The dynamic global commands push `nav.drillOut` (cua: Escape) **before** the static `app.dismiss` (cua: Escape) so `nav.drillOut` claims Escape (`buildDynamicGlobalCommands` ordering is load-bearing — see comment block at lines 497–509).
+
+2. **`nav.drillOut.execute`** (`app-shell.tsx:354`):
+   - Reads `actions.focusedKey()`.
+   - If `null`: calls `refs.dismissRef.current()` (i.e. dispatches `app.dismiss`) and returns.
+   - Otherwise calls `actions.drillOut(key)` (Tauri → `swissarmyhammer_focus::SpatialRegistry::drill_out`).
+   - Non-null moniker result → `setFocus(moniker)` (move focus to parent zone).
+   - **Null result → `refs.dismissRef.current()`** (fall through to `app.dismiss`).
+
+3. **`SpatialRegistry::drill_out`** (`swissarmyhammer-focus/src/registry.rs:363`): returns `entry.parent_zone()` mapped to a moniker. **For a zone with `parent_zone = None` (the panel zone, registered directly under the inspector layer) this returns `None`** — the layer-root case.
+
+4. **`app.dismiss` → `DismissCmd::execute`** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/app_commands.rs:138`): Layer 1 closes the palette if open; **Layer 2 calls `ui.inspector_close(window_label)`** which pops the topmost panel from `inspector_stack`.
+
+5. **`inspectors-container.tsx`** subscribes to `inspector_stack` via `useUIState`, syncs `panelStack` state, and unmounts the topmost `<InspectorPanel>` when the stack shrinks. The `<FocusLayer name="inspector">` unmounts when the stack empties, popping the layer in the Rust registry and emitting the parent layer's `last_focused`.
+
+The links are all there. The bug is that the chain isn't actually firing in production. The work here is a **diagnose-and-fix**, not a redesign — pin the failure point with a real-tree test and fix the broken link.
+
+## Likely failure points (to investigate, not assume)
+
+- **Panel-zone registration**: `InspectorPanel` renders `<FocusZone moniker={panelMoniker} className="min-h-full">` inside `<SlidePanel>`. The panel zone reads `useParentZoneKey()` to populate its `parent_zone` field. If the surrounding tree contains an unintended `<FocusZone>` ancestor (e.g. a hidden chrome wrap, a future card wraps the SlidePanel content), the panel ends up with a non-null `parent_zone` pointing at that ancestor. Drill-out then walks zones inside the inspector layer instead of returning `None`, and `nav.drillOut` never falls through.
+
+- **Dispatcher fall-through in `nav.drillOut`**: the `null → dismissRef.current()` branch may not actually fire — verify the closure runs and the awaited dispatch resolves.
+
+- **`DismissCmd::execute` reachability**: confirm `useDispatchCommand("app.dismiss")` reaches `DismissCmd::execute` with a `CommandContext` whose `ui_state` and `window_label_from_scope()` are populated correctly. A null window label would short-circuit the inspector branch.
+
+- **Focus state when inspector opens**: when the user clicks the inspect button in the navbar, `ui.inspect` opens the panel but does it move spatial focus into the panel? If focus stays on the navbar button, drill-out runs from the navbar key, which has nothing to do with the inspector; it walks navbar zones, eventually hits null, and *should* fall through to `app.dismiss` — verify this branch actually fires.
+
+## Approach
+
+Build this on top of the realistic-fixture test infrastructure being introduced by **`01KQ7STZN3G5N2WB3FF4PM4DKX`** (directional nav fixtures under `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/fixtures/`). Reuse the same fixture builder so the registry shape under test matches the production tree the user actually navigates.
+
+Pin the failure in three layers, each at its own seam:
+
+- **Kernel layer** — Rust integration test against a realistic `SpatialRegistry`.
+- **Backend command layer** — Rust integration test of `DismissCmd::execute` against a realistic `CommandContext` + `UIState` with `inspector_stack` populated.
+- **Frontend chain** — browser test that mounts the production provider stack, opens an inspector panel, presses Escape on the focused scope, and asserts the panel is removed from `UIState` (not by mocking the dispatcher — by asserting the observable end state).
+
+Fix whichever seam is broken; if more than one is broken, fix each.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Pressing **Escape** with one inspector panel open closes that panel (the panel disappears, `inspector_stack` empties, the inspector layer pops, focus restores to the parent layer's `last_focused`). Asserted by automated tests at each seam below — no manual smoke step.
+- [ ] Pressing **Escape** with two inspector panels open closes the topmost panel only. Repeating closes the next. After both close, the inspector layer unmounts.
+- [ ] Pressing **Escape** with the inspector closed and no palette open behaves as before — drills out within the focused layer or no-ops at the layer root. (Regression guard: don't break the existing Escape behavior for non-inspector cases.)
+- [ ] Pressing **Escape** with the inspector open and the palette open closes the **palette** first (Layer 1 of `DismissCmd`). The inspector remains open. A second Escape closes the inspector.
+
+## Tests
+
+All tests are automated. No manual verification.
+
+### Rust kernel — `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/inspector_dismiss.rs` (new file)
+
+Builds a realistic registry mirroring the production tree: window-root layer with a board zone containing a column zone containing a card leaf; inspector layer (parent = window) with a panel zone (`parent_zone = None`).
+
+- [ ] `drill_out_panel_zone_returns_none` — `drill_out(panel_zone_key)` returns `None`. (If this fails, the panel zone has the wrong `parent_zone` in production — check `InspectorPanel`'s tree for an unintended ancestor `<FocusZone>`.)
+- [ ] `drill_out_leaf_inside_panel_returns_panel_moniker` — focus on a leaf inside the panel; `drill_out` returns the panel moniker. (Regression guard for the in-panel walk.)
+- [ ] `drill_out_panel_with_no_inspector_layer_does_not_collapse_to_window` — register only a window layer with a board, no inspector layer; drill-out from the topmost board-side scope returns `None` and does not somehow reach into a non-existent inspector. (Regression guard for layer isolation.)
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus --test inspector_dismiss` — all three pass.
+
+### Rust backend — `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/dismiss_inspector_integration.rs` (new file)
+
+Builds a realistic `CommandContext` with a `UIState` whose `inspector_stack` has one entry for the active window. Dispatches `app.dismiss`.
+
+- [ ] `dismiss_with_inspector_open_pops_topmost_panel` — `DismissCmd::execute` returns a `UIStateChange` for `inspector_close`; `UIState::inspector_stack(window_label)` is empty afterward.
+- [ ] `dismiss_with_two_panels_open_pops_topmost_only` — initial stack is `[panel:task:a, panel:task:b]`; dispatch closes `panel:task:b`; stack is `[panel:task:a]`.
+- [ ] `dismiss_with_palette_and_inspector_open_closes_palette_first` — `palette_open=true` and `inspector_stack` non-empty; dispatch closes the palette, leaves the stack untouched.
+- [ ] `dismiss_with_nothing_open_returns_null` — empty stack, palette closed; dispatch returns `Value::Null` and produces no `UIStateChange`. (Regression guard.)
+
+Test command: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban --test dismiss_inspector_integration` — all four pass.
+
+### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/inspector-dismiss.browser.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+Mounts the production provider stack (`<DiagErrorBoundary>` → `<SpatialFocusProvider>` → `<FocusLayer name="window">` → `<CommandBusyProvider>` → `<RustEngineContainer>` → `<WindowContainer>` → ...). Drives the real Tauri command bridge against a per-test backend the existing browser tests already use. Opens an inspector panel by dispatching `ui.inspect` against a real entity moniker (no mocked dispatcher). Asserts on observable `UIState`, not on internal calls.
+
+- [ ] `escape_with_panel_focused_closes_inspector` — open inspector for a card, focus the panel zone, fire `keydown { key: "Escape" }` on `document`, await one tick, assert `UIState.windows[label].inspector_stack` is empty.
+- [ ] `escape_with_field_inside_panel_focused_eventually_closes_inspector` — open inspector, focus an inspector field zone inside the panel; first Escape moves focus to the panel zone (`useFocusedScope` reports `panel:...`); second Escape empties the inspector stack.
+- [ ] `escape_with_inspector_open_and_focus_outside_panel_closes_inspector` — open inspector but leave focus on a board card; one Escape closes the inspector via fall-through to `app.dismiss` (drill-out from the card returns column → continues until a layer-root scope returns null; for the simple "card has parent column" case, the test asserts the user can press Escape twice and the inspector is closed by the second press, OR — if the unified policy lands first — by the first press once drill-out at the layer root returns null).
+
+Test command: `bun run test:browser inspector-dismiss.browser.test.tsx` — all three pass.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing tests first against the real production wiring, run them, identify which seam fails, fix that seam.
+- Build on top of the realistic-fixture infrastructure in `01KQ7STZN3G5N2WB3FF4PM4DKX` rather than rolling a parallel JS-shadow registry. (If `01KQ7STZN3G5N2WB3FF4PM4DKX` hasn't introduced the fixture builder yet when this task starts, build the minimal piece you need for the kernel test and leave a note; the directional-nav card will subsume it on the next pass.)
+- Single ticket — do not split this into "one card per Escape variant". Every variant exercises the same chain. If you discover the chain is more than one bug, fix each at its seam in the same PR.
